title: pseudopanax gilliesii
intro: Pseudopanax gilliesii, also known as the Lancewood or Pittosporum lanceolatum, is a plant native to New Zealand, characterized by its lance-shaped leaves and small, fragrant flowers. The plant has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of essential oils such as limonene and pinene. The health benefits of Pseudopanax gilliesii include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant is often prepared in herbal teas or infused in oils for its medicinal uses, although further research is needed to fully understand its potential benefits and applications.
